fingerprints.method_in_state: N/A — Massachusetts uses a CORI (Criminal Offender Record Information) background check instead of fingerprinting
fingerprints.method_out_of_state: N/A — CORI Acknowledgement Form must be notarized and uploaded to the online application

seo.title: Massachusetts LPN License by Endorsement — Requirements & Fees (2026)
seo.description: Apply for a Massachusetts Practical Nurse (PN) license by reciprocity through the Board of Registration in Nursing. $305 estimated total fee. COG form, Nursys verification, CE courses, and notarized CORI required.
seo.keywords: Massachusetts LPN license, MA practical nurse license, Massachusetts nursing license by reciprocity, MA Board of Registration in Nursing, LPN endorsement Massachusetts

board.name: Board of Registration in Nursing
board.mailing_address:
Board of Registration in Nursing
250 Washington Street
Boston, MA 02108

license_types.title: Practical Nurse (PN) License by Reciprocity
license_types.description: The endorsement pathway for LPNs already licensed in another state. Requires Nursys license verification, Certification of Graduation (COG) form, official transcripts, CE courses (Domestic & Sexual Violence + Cognitive Impairment), a notarized CORI form, and a 2x2 passport-style photo.

steps.title: Create a Plus Portal Account
steps.description: Register for an account on the Massachusetts Department of Public Health's Health Professions Licensing System, known as the Plus Portal, and select the 'Board of Registration in Nursing' to begin your application.

steps.title: Complete and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Fill out the LPN application form on the Plus Portal, provide all required information and documentation, and pay the initial licensure application fee.

steps.title: Register for NCLEX-PN and Submit Transcripts
steps.description: Register with Pearson VUE for the NCLEX-PN examination and arrange for your official transcripts to be sent directly from your approved nursing program to the Massachusetts Board of Registration in Nursing.

steps.title: Pass the NCLEX-PN Examination
steps.description: Schedule and successfully pass the NCLEX-PN examination. The Board of Registration in Nursing will issue your LPN license once all application requirements are met and you have passed the exam.

documents.detail: Must be completed by your nursing program administrator and mailed directly to PCS. Use the out-of-state form if applicable.
Sent directly to PCS electronically (transcripts@pcshq.com via Parchment or National Student Clearinghouse) or by mail in a sealed envelope.
For all professional nursing licenses held (RN, PN, APRN), active or inactive.
Must be manually signed (no electronic signatures), notarized, with matching dates on pages 1 and 3. No cross-outs or white-out.
Color photo, white background, taken within the last year. Face and shoulders clearly visible.
Free online training (Chapter 260). Save certificate for audit purposes.
Active military, military spouse, or veteran status requires a notarized affidavit form.

provisional_text: Massachusetts does not issue a provisional or temporary LPN license for endorsement applicants. Applications are reviewed only after all required documents and fees are received by PCS.
reciprocity_text: Massachusetts grants LPN licensure by reciprocity. Out-of-state LPNs must submit via the Mass.gov online system, provide Nursys verification of all nursing licenses held, submit a Certification of Graduation (COG) form and official transcripts through PCS, complete mandatory CE courses, and upload a notarized CORI form. Foreign-educated nurses face an additional 6–8 week delay and must complete a credential evaluation through TruMerit, Josef Silny & Associates, or The Evaluation Company.

faqs.question: How much does a Massachusetts LPN license by endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The total estimated cost is $305: $275 for the application fee, plus $30 for Nursys license verification. Additional costs may apply for transcripts, notarization, and CE courses depending on the vendor.

faqs.question: What is the COG form and why is it so important?
faqs.answer: The Certification of Graduation (COG) form is the most important and time-sensitive document. It must be completed by the program administrator at your original nursing school and mailed directly to PCS. Your application will not be reviewed until PCS receives it.

faqs.question: Can I submit the COG form or transcripts myself?
faqs.answer: No. PCS will not accept the COG form or transcripts directly from the applicant. Both must be sent directly from your educational institution.

faqs.question: Do I need fingerprints for a Massachusetts nursing license?
faqs.answer: No. Massachusetts uses a CORI (Criminal Offender Record Information) background check instead of fingerprinting. You must submit a notarized CORI Acknowledgement Form with your application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to get a Massachusetts LPN license?
faqs.answer: Standard processing is 6 to 8 weeks after PCS receives all required documents. Delays are common if the COG form or transcripts are missing. Foreign-educated nurses may experience additional delays.

faqs.question: Will I receive a paper license in the mail?
faqs.answer: No. A paper license will NOT be mailed. You and your employer can print a copy of your license verification from the Board's website at checkahealthlicense.mass.gov.

official_links.registration_steps: Create an account on the Plus Portal, select the Board of Registration in Nursing, and initiate a new LPN application.
